generated_not_exported.likely_meaning: The output did not clear the bar for real work
generated_not_exported.cs_action: Within 24 hours, ask the user to mark the first unusable line or number. Diagnose whether the blocker was unsupported sourcing, wrong format for the reviewer, weak explanation, or a number they could not tie back to the file.

saved_not_rerun.signal: Same job appears once but does not repeat
saved_not_rerun.likely_meaning: The first run was interesting, but it did not attach to a live operating cadence
saved_not_rerun.cs_action: Find the next real deadline for the same job: next earnings print, IC meeting, board update, close cycle, or diligence sprint. Set up the next run before that date and remove any steps that made the first run feel too broad.

heavy_overrides.likely_meaning: Endex is learning the wrong version of how the customer wants the work done
heavy_overrides.cs_action: Pull the before/after diff. Ask which edits are personal style, which are firm standard, and which are factual corrections. Convert firm-standard edits into saved preferences; route factual corrections to product or evals.

single_champion.signal: One active user and no reviewer adoption
single_champion.likely_meaning: The account can still die if the champion gets busy, leaves, or cannot persuade the reviewer
single_champion.cs_action: Ask the champion who consumes the output today. Create a version for that person: PM summary, MD memo, CFO variance note, or principal review packet. Get that reviewer into the next run.

expansion_ready.signal: Same work repeated, saved preferences reused, reviewer adoption, and exports
expansion_ready.likely_meaning: Endex has earned the right to ask for more surface area
expansion_ready.cs_action: Do not pitch a platform expansion yet. Ask what happens immediately before or after this repeated job. Expand to the adjacent step, adjacent coverage list, or adjacent reviewer team while usage is still fresh.
